settings.text: Eight people who've shipped a lot of software.

Codebase was founded in 2019 by two engineers who'd spent the previous decade building product at Stripe, Figma, and a handful of smaller European startups. We started the studio because we wanted to keep doing deep product work without the organisational gravity that comes with a fifty-person agency.

Today we're eight — six engineers, two product designers, zero account managers. Every project is led by one of us and staffed with two to four people. We take two projects at a time. That's the ceiling we've decided to keep.

settings.name: Mira Hartmann
settings.bio: Founded Codebase in 2019. Ten years at Stripe before that, shipping core billing and internal product platforms. Leads the studio's most architecturally complex engagements.

settings.name: Alex Kowalski
settings.bio: Leads product design across every engagement. Previously at Figma and a handful of consumer startups. Runs the studio's design-system practice and does most of the client-facing discovery work.

settings.name: Yuki Tanaka
settings.bio: Senior front-end engineer focused on data-dense interfaces. Previously built reporting tools at Linear. Knows more about virtual scroll than is reasonable.

settings.name: Dimitri Andreou
settings.bio: Senior back-end engineer. Rust and Postgres by preference, Node when the project needs it. Previously at Supabase and a European fintech infrastructure team.

settings.name: Imani Okonkwo
settings.bio: Full-stack senior engineer and the lead on most of the studio's MVP sprints. Previously shipped zero-to-one products at two Y Combinator startups.

settings.name: Luca Bianchi
settings.bio: Engineer with a focus on monorepo tooling, typed APIs, and the ergonomics of long-lived codebases. Joined from a Turin-based platform engineering team in 2023.

settings.name: Sarah Holm
settings.bio: Full-stack engineer with a specialty in auth and fine-grained permissions. Previously built IAM infrastructure at a compliance SaaS. The person you want on the account-security conversation.

settings.name: Theo Laurent
settings.bio: Product designer with a research background. Runs the studio's early-stage discovery and user-research work. Writes design documentation your engineers will actually read.

settings.title: Studio founded
settings.description: Mira and Alex leave their previous engineering roles and open Codebase in a small office in Berlin-Mitte. First two clients are referrals from their previous companies.

settings.title: First ten engagements
settings.description: The studio crosses ten shipped projects and adds Yuki and Dimitri as the first full-time senior engineers. Moves to a larger space near Kottbusser Tor.

settings.title: Retainer practice launched
settings.description: After three years of repeated requests, the studio launches an on-call engineering retainer for past clients who want a senior engineer on speed-dial.

settings.title: Design practice formalized
settings.description: Theo joins as a dedicated product designer. Every engagement now includes design from discovery through launch, with no separate design vendor handoff.

settings.title: Studio at eight
settings.description: The team settles at eight people. A deliberate ceiling — big enough to staff two projects at once, small enough that everyone knows the codebase of everything we ship.

settings.text: You'll talk to Mira or Alex on the first call. Reply within two business days.
